codeReview.summary: This was a clippy fix session - the cross_memory_integration integration test already existed and was passing. The worker fixed 3 clippy warnings: (1) simplified boolean expression at line 279, (2) prefixed unused `memory_blocks` with underscore at line 334, (3) prefixed unused `result` with underscore at line 510. All 4 integration tests pass, crate clippy passes, workspace clippy passes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature specifies skillName='rust-worker' but the skill file ~/.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md does not exist. The worker transcript shows 'mission-worker-base' and 'rust-worker' were attempted, but only 'rust' skill exists at ~/.factory/skills/rust/SKILL.md. The work was completed successfully despite this mismatch - the worker followed TDD pattern and fixed clippy warnings correctly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature has skillName='rust-worker' but ls ~/.factory/skills/ shows 'rust' not 'rust-worker'. Transcript shows Skill tool calls for 'mission-worker-base' and 'rust-worker' - the latter failed silently.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The actual memory integration tests were written in a prior worker session (7fc50905-b0d1-4e7f-8593-e81d3f730937). This session (06614852) only fixed clippy warnings. The skillFeedback indicates procedure was followed, but there's no rust-worker SKILL.md to reference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows workerSessionIds: ['7fc50905...', '06614852...']. Handoff commit 4671ca4 only touches 8 lines in cross_memory_integration.rs for clippy fixes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ExecutionController test (test_execution_controller_with_memory_context) has a TODO comment noting that memory_blocks are NOT wired from context to GeneratorAgent. The test passes but doesn't assert memory context presence - it just verifies prompts were captured. This is a known limitation documented in the test itself.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cross_memory_integration.rs lines 361-370: 'Note: The ExecutionController currently does NOT wire memory_blocks from context to GeneratorAgent. This test verifies that the LLM is being called, but the memory context assertion is disabled until ExecutionController memory integration is implemented.'

summary: Review PASSED. This was a clippy fix session - 3 warnings fixed in cross_memory_integration.rs (simplified boolean expression, prefixed 2 unused variables with underscore). The 4 integration tests already existed and pass. No code issues found. One shared state observation: the rust-worker skill file referenced in the feature doesn't exist (only 'rust' skill exists), which the worker worked around successfully.
